content.text: Azure AI Foundry (formerly Azure AI Studio) is Microsoft's unified platform for building, deploying, and managing AI applications on Azure. Claude is available in the Foundry Model Catalog, allowing Azure customers to deploy and call Claude without leaving the Azure ecosystem.
Microsoft Foundry provides two deployment modes for Claude:
(reserved capacity for predictable latency). The choice between them depends on your traffic pattern and SLA requirements.

your organization is Azure-first, has existing Azure consumption commitments, uses Azure OpenAI Service today and wants to add Claude alongside it, needs HIPAA BAA or FedRAMP authorization through Microsoft, or your compliance team has already evaluated Azure's security controls.

attrs.alt: Microsoft Foundry architecture: Your App, Azure AI Foundry, Claude model, Microsoft Entra auth, Private Endpoint, and deployment modes

content.text: Creating a Deployment in Azure AI Foundry
Before you can call Claude, you need a Foundry project with a Claude deployment. This is a one-time setup step per environment.
Select your Claude model (e.g. Claude Opus 4.8) and click

# Install the Azure AI CLI extension
az extension add --name ml

# Create a Foundry project (if needed)
az ml workspace create   --name my-foundry-project   --resource-group my-rg   --kind hub

# Deploy Claude (serverless)
az ml model deploy   --name claude-opus-4-8-deployment   --model anthropic-claude-opus-4-8   --instance-type Standard_DS3_v2   --resource-group my-rg   --workspace-name my-foundry-project
(Managed Identity, the production-grade approach that avoids storing secrets).

import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic(
    api_key="YOUR_FOUNDRY_API_KEY",         # from deployment details
    base_url="https://YOUR_ENDPOINT.openai.azure.com/",   # your Foundry endpoint
)

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-opus-4-8",                # model name as configured in Foundry
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello from Azure AI Foundry!"}],
)
print(message.content[0].text)
For production workloads on Azure VM, App Service, AKS, or Azure Functions, use Managed Identity to avoid storing API keys. Assign the
role to the managed identity, then retrieve a token at runtime:

from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential
from azure.ai.inference import ChatCompletionsClient
from azure.ai.inference.models import SystemMessage, UserMessage

# DefaultAzureCredential picks up Managed Identity automatically on Azure
credential = DefaultAzureCredential()

client = ChatCompletionsClient(
    endpoint="https://YOUR_ENDPOINT.openai.azure.com/",
    credential=credential,
)

response = client.complete(
    model="claude-opus-4-8",
    messages=[
        SystemMessage(content="You are a helpful assistant."),
        UserMessage(content="Explain Managed Identity in 2 sentences."),
    ],
    max_tokens=256,
)
print(response.choices[0].message.content)
for the Managed Identity approach. The
class chains through Managed Identity, environment variables, Azure CLI credentials, and VS Code credentials — whichever is present in the environment.
Lower (you pay only for tokens used)
Higher (capacity reserved whether used or not)
Lower per token at sustained high volume

Start with Serverless during development and testing. Switch to Provisioned Throughput when you can measure your steady-state token throughput and the reserved capacity becomes cheaper than per-token billing.
Using the Anthropic SDK with Foundry
The Anthropic Python SDK can be pointed at a Foundry endpoint by overriding
. This is the same SDK you would use for the Direct API — only the constructor changes.

import anthropic
import os

# Read credentials from environment variables
FOUNDRY_ENDPOINT = os.environ["AZURE_FOUNDRY_ENDPOINT"]    # https://YOUR.openai.azure.com/
FOUNDRY_API_KEY  = os.environ["AZURE_FOUNDRY_API_KEY"]

client = anthropic.Anthropic(
    api_key=FOUNDRY_API_KEY,
    base_url=FOUNDRY_ENDPOINT,
)

# Multi-turn conversation
messages = []

def chat(user_input: str) -> str:
    messages.append({"role": "user", "content": user_input})
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-opus-4-8",
        max_tokens=1024,
        messages=messages,
    )
    reply = response.content[0].text
    messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": reply})
    return reply

print(chat("What is Azure AI Foundry?"))
print(chat("How is it different from Azure OpenAI Service?"))
For strict network isolation, create an
for your AI Foundry resource. Traffic from your VNet to Foundry then flows through Microsoft's backbone — the endpoint never resolves to a public IP.

# Create a private endpoint for AI Foundry
az network private-endpoint create   --name foundry-private-ep   --resource-group my-rg   --vnet-name my-vnet   --subnet my-subnet   --private-connection-resource-id /subscriptions/SUBSCRIPTION_ID/resourceGroups/my-rg/providers/Microsoft.MachineLearningServices/workspaces/my-foundry-project   --connection-name foundry-connection   --group-id amlworkspace

# Create Private DNS zone for AI Foundry
az network private-dns zone create   --resource-group my-rg   --name "privatelink.api.azureml.ms"

az network private-dns link vnet create   --resource-group my-rg   --zone-name "privatelink.api.azureml.ms"   --name my-vnet-link   --virtual-network my-vnet   --registration-enabled false
With the private endpoint in place, requests from resources in
resolve to the private IP address and never leave the Azure network — satisfying requirements for air-gapped or highly regulated deployments.
Foundry automatically integrates with Azure Monitor. Enable diagnostic settings on your AI Foundry resource to route metrics and logs to Log Analytics, Event Hubs, or Storage.

from azure.monitor.query import MetricsQueryClient, MetricAggregationType
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential
from datetime import timedelta

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
client = MetricsQueryClient(credential)

resource_id = "/subscriptions/SUBSCRIPTION_ID/resourceGroups/my-rg/providers/Microsoft.MachineLearningServices/workspaces/my-foundry-project"

result = client.query_resource(
    resource_id,
    metric_names=["RequestCount", "Latency"],
    timespan=timedelta(hours=1),
    granularity=timedelta(minutes=5),
    aggregations=[MetricAggregationType.COUNT, MetricAggregationType.AVERAGE],
)

for metric in result.metrics:
    print(f"{metric.name}:")
    for ts in metric.timeseries:
        for point in ts.data:
            print(f"  {point.timestamp}: {point.count or point.average}")
. Set Azure Monitor Alerts to page on-call when error rate exceeds 1% over a 5-minute window.
Azure AI Foundry inherits Azure's compliance certifications. Key ones for Claude deployments:
Information security management — baseline for enterprise deployments
Security and availability controls — reports available in Azure Trust Center
Sign a BAA with Microsoft; Foundry is covered under Azure's HIPAA coverage
Available in Azure Government (GovCloud) regions for US federal workloads
Microsoft's DPA covers AI Foundry as a data processor — data stays in the Azure region you deploy to
Request Azure compliance reports from the
(servicetrust.microsoft.com). These are the audit reports you share with your compliance team when evaluating Claude on Foundry for regulated workloads.
Streaming works with both the Anthropic SDK and the Azure AI Inference SDK. Using the Anthropic SDK approach (with
override), the streaming interface is identical to the Direct API:

import anthropic
import os

client = anthropic.Anthropic(
    api_key=os.environ["AZURE_FOUNDRY_API_KEY"],
    base_url=os.environ["AZURE_FOUNDRY_ENDPOINT"],
)

with client.messages.stream(
    model="claude-opus-4-8",
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Describe Azure AI Foundry in detail."}],
) as stream:
    for text in stream.text_stream:
        print(text, end="", flush=True)
print()
For the Azure AI Inference SDK, use
Azure AI Foundry returns standard HTTP error codes. When using the Anthropic SDK with
pointing to Foundry, errors surface as the usual
types. When using the Azure AI Inference SDK directly, errors come as
Wait and retry with exponential backoff
Invalid API key or Entra token expired
Refresh credentials; check Managed Identity assignment
Verify deployment name and endpoint URL
Retry with backoff; check Azure Service Health
Microsoft Foundry is most compelling when you combine Claude with other Azure services. Three high-value integration patterns:

from azure.storage.blob import BlobServiceClient
import anthropic, os

blob_client = BlobServiceClient.from_connection_string(
    os.environ["AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING"]
)
claude = anthropic.Anthropic(
    api_key=os.environ["AZURE_FOUNDRY_API_KEY"],
    base_url=os.environ["AZURE_FOUNDRY_ENDPOINT"],
)

# Download and summarize a document
container = blob_client.get_container_client("documents")
blob_data = container.get_blob_client("contract.txt").download_blob().readall().decode()

response = claude.messages.create(
    model="claude-opus-4-8",
    max_tokens=512,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": f"Summarize this contract:\n\n{blob_data}"}],
)
print(response.content[0].text)

import azure.functions as func
import anthropic, os, json

app = func.FunctionApp()

@app.function_name(name="ClassifyEmail")
@app.service_bus_queue_trigger(
    arg_name="msg",
    queue_name="incoming-emails",
    connection="SERVICE_BUS_CONNECTION",
)
def classify_email(msg: func.ServiceBusMessage) -> None:
    email_body = msg.get_body().decode()

    client = anthropic.Anthropic(
        api_key=os.environ["AZURE_FOUNDRY_API_KEY"],
        base_url=os.environ["AZURE_FOUNDRY_ENDPOINT"],
    )
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5",   # fast + cheap for classification
        max_tokens=20,
        system="Reply with exactly one word: urgent, normal, or spam.",
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": email_body}],
    )
    priority = response.content[0].text.strip().lower()
    print(f"Email classified as: {priority}")
    # Route to Azure Service Bus topic based on priority
    # ... (publish to next queue)
Azure AI Search (formerly Cognitive Search) provides a managed vector index. You can build a Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipeline using Azure AI Search for retrieval and Claude for generation:

from azure.search.documents import SearchClient
from azure.core.credentials import AzureKeyCredential
import anthropic, os

search = SearchClient(
    endpoint=os.environ["SEARCH_ENDPOINT"],
    index_name="knowledge-base",
    credential=AzureKeyCredential(os.environ["SEARCH_KEY"]),
)
claude = anthropic.Anthropic(
    api_key=os.environ["AZURE_FOUNDRY_API_KEY"],
    base_url=os.environ["AZURE_FOUNDRY_ENDPOINT"],
)

def rag_answer(question: str) -> str:
    # 1. Retrieve relevant chunks
    results = search.search(question, top=3)
    context = "\n\n".join(r["content"] for r in results)

    # 2. Generate answer with Claude
    response = claude.messages.create(
        model="claude-opus-4-8",
        max_tokens=512,
        system="Answer only from the provided context. If the answer is not in the context, say so.",
        messages=[
            {"role": "user", "content": f"Context:\n{context}\n\nQuestion: {question}"},
        ],
    )
    return response.content[0].text

print(rag_answer("What is our return policy?"))
Comparing Foundry to Bedrock and Vertex AI
All three platforms satisfy comparable compliance requirements. The deciding factor is usually which cloud provider your organization already uses and where the rest of your data stack lives.

Microsoft Azure AI Foundry makes Claude available within the Azure ecosystem, giving Azure-native organizations a path to Claude with Microsoft's compliance certifications, billing, monitoring, and network security. Serverless deployment is ideal for variable traffic; Provisioned Throughput is better for predictable production load. Authentication should use Managed Identity (not API keys) in production environments. Private Endpoints eliminate public-internet exposure for regulated deployments. The Anthropic SDK works with Foundry by overriding
— the rest of the API is identical to Direct API calls.
